Equivalent (JP): システムモデリング言語 (SysML)
Definition (EN): The System Modeling Language (SysML) contains concepts that are used to model systems, their components, and the external environment in a context.
Context/Example (EN): The purpose of this standard is to specify the Systems Modeling Language™ (SysML), to guide the implementation of conformant modeling tools, and to provide the basis for the development of material and other resources to train users in the application of SysML.

Equivalent (JP): カーネルモデリング言語 (KerML)
Definition (EN): It extends the Kernel Modeling Language (KerML) as specified in the KerML specification [KerML].
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses some elements of KerML, but most SysML elements are specializations of KerML elements.

Definition (EN): Elements and relationships that define the basic graph structure of a model (see 7.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Elements and relationships that define the basic graph structure of a model (see 7.2 ).

Definition (EN): Dependencies between modeling elements (see 7.3 ).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Dependencies between modeling elements (see 7.3 ).

Definition (EN): Annotations for attaching metadata to a model, including comments and textual representations (see 7.4 ).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Annotations for attaching metadata to a model, including comments and textual representations (see 7.4 ).

Definition (EN): Namespaces that contain and name elements, and, particularly, packages used to organize the elements in a model (see 7.5 ).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Namespaces that contain and name elements, and, particularly, packages used to organize the elements in a model (see 7.5 ).

Definition (EN): Namespaces that contain and name elements, and, particularly, packages used to organize the elements in a model (see 7.5 ).
Context/Example (EN): Namespaces that contain and name elements, and, particularly, packages used to organize the elements in a model (see 7.5 ).

Definition (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).

Definition (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).
Context/Example (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).

Definition (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).
Context/Example (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).

Context/Example (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).

Equivalent (JP): フィーチャタイピング
Definition (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).
Context/Example (EN): Specialization of elements that specify types, including subclassification, subsetting, redefinition and feature typing (see 7.6 ).

Definition (EN): Expressions can be used to specify calculations, case results, constraints and formal requirements.
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Expressions can be used to specify calculations, case results, constraints and formal requirements.

Definition (EN): The general pattern of definition and usage, which is applied to many of the SysML language constructs (see 7.6 ).
Context/Example (EN): Fundamental aspects of constructing a model, including: The general pattern of definition and usage, which is applied to many of the SysML language constructs (see 7.6 ).

Definition (EN): A usage element can be further specialized for its specific context.
Context/Example (EN): The pattern of definition and usage elements facilitates model reuse, such that a concept can be defined once and then used in many different contexts. A usage element can be further specialized for its specific context.

Definition (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.
Context/Example (EN): Fundamental aspects of constructing a model, including: The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.

Equivalent (JP): バリエーションポイント
Definition (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.

Definition (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of variability, which includes the definition of variation points within a model where choices can be made to select a specific variant, and the selection of a particular variant may constrain the allowable choices at other variation points.

Definition (EN): Attributes that specify characteristics of something that can be defined by simple or compound data types, and dimensional quantities such as mass, length, etc. (see 7.7 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of attributive information about things, including: Attributes that specify characteristics of something that can be defined by simple or compound data types, and dimensional quantities such as mass, length, etc. (see 7.7 ).

Definition (EN): Enumerations that are attributes restricted to a specified set of enumerated values (see 7.8 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of attributive information about things, including: Enumerations that are attributes restricted to a specified set of enumerated values (see 7.8 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of occurrences with temporal and spatial extent. Temporal extent enables an occurrence to be represented at specific points in time, over a duration in time, or over an entire lifetime. Spatial extent enables an occurrence to be represented at a position and orientation with respect to a coordinate frame, and to have a shape and size (see 7.9 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of occurrences with temporal and spatial extent. Temporal extent enables an occurrence to be represented at specific points in time, over a duration in time, or over an entire lifetime. Spatial extent enables an occurrence to be represented at a position and orientation with respect to a coordinate frame, and to have a shape and size (see 7.9 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of individuals with specific identities (see 7.9 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of individuals with specific identities (see 7.9 ).

Definition (EN): Items that may flow through a process or system or be stored by a system (see 7.10 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Items that may flow through a process or system or be stored by a system (see 7.10 ).

Definition (EN): Parts that are the foundational units of structure, which can be composed and interconnected, to form composite parts and entire systems (see 7.11 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Parts that are the foundational units of structure, which can be composed and interconnected, to form composite parts and entire systems (see 7.11 ).

Definition (EN): Ports that define connection points on parts that enable interactions between parts (see 7.12 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Ports that define connection points on parts that enable interactions between parts (see 7.12 ).

Definition (EN): Connections (see 7.13 ) and interfaces (see 7.14 ) that define how parts and ports are interconnected.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Connections (see 7.13 ) and interfaces (see 7.14 ) that define how parts and ports are interconnected.

Definition (EN): Connections (see 7.13 ) and interfaces (see 7.14 ) that define how parts and ports are interconnected.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Connections (see 7.13 ) and interfaces (see 7.14 ) that define how parts and ports are interconnected.

Definition (EN): Allocations that assign responsibility for realizing the features of one element by another element (see 7.15 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of structure to represent how parts are decomposed, interconnected and classified, and includes: Allocations that assign responsibility for realizing the features of one element by another element (see 7.15 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of behavior, which specifies how parts interact and includes: Flows of values and items between parts and actions (see 7.16 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of behavior, which specifies how parts interact and includes: Flows of values and items between parts and actions (see 7.16 ).

Definition (EN): Flows of values and items between parts and actions (see 7.16 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of behavior, which specifies how parts interact and includes: Flows of values and items between parts and actions (see 7.16 ).

Definition (EN): Actions performed by a part, including their temporal ordering (see 7.17 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of behavior, which specifies how parts interact and includes: Actions performed by a part, including their temporal ordering (see 7.17 ).

Definition (EN): States exhibited by a part, the allowable transitions between those states, and the actions enabled in a state or during a transition (see 7.18 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of behavior, which specifies how parts interact and includes: States exhibited by a part, the allowable transitions between those states, and the actions enabled in a state or during a transition (see 7.18 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of calculations that are parameterized expressions that can be evaluated to produce specific results (see 7.19 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of calculations that are parameterized expressions that can be evaluated to produce specific results (see 7.19 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of constraints, which specify conditions that can be evaluated as true or false, or asserted to be true or false (see 7.20 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of constraints, which specify conditions that can be evaluated as true or false, or asserted to be true or false (see 7.20 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of requirements, which is a special kind of constraint that a subject must satisfy to be a valid solution (see 7.21 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of requirements, which is a special kind of constraint that a subject must satisfy to be a valid solution (see 7.21 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of cases, which define the steps required to produce a desired result relative to a subject, possibly also involving external actors, to achieve a specific objective (see 7.22 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of cases, which define the steps required to produce a desired result relative to a subject, possibly also involving external actors, to achieve a specific objective (see 7.22 ).

Definition (EN): Analysis cases, whose steps are the actions necessary to analyze a subject (see 7.23 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of cases, which define the steps required to produce a desired result relative to a subject, possibly also involving external actors, to achieve a specific objective (see 7.22 ), including: Analysis cases, whose steps are the actions necessary to analyze a subject (see 7.23 ).

Definition (EN): Verification cases, whose objective is to verify how a requirement is satisfied by the subject (see 7.24 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of cases, which define the steps required to produce a desired result relative to a subject, possibly also involving external actors, to achieve a specific objective (see 7.22 ), including: Verification cases, whose objective is to verify how a requirement is satisfied by the subject (see 7.24 ).

Definition (EN): Use cases, that specify required behavior of the subject with the objective of providing a measurable benefit to one or more external actors (see 7.25 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of cases, which define the steps required to produce a desired result relative to a subject, possibly also involving external actors, to achieve a specific objective (see 7.22 ), including: Use cases, that specify required behavior of the subject with the objective of providing a measurable benefit to one or more external actors (see 7.25 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of viewpoints that specify information of interest by a set of stakeholders, and views that specify a query of the model, and a rendering of the query results, that is intended to satisfy a particular viewpoint (see 7.26 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of viewpoints that specify information of interest by a set of stakeholders, and views that specify a query of the model, and a rendering of the query results, that is intended to satisfy a particular viewpoint (see 7.26 ).

Definition (EN): The modeling of viewpoints that specify information of interest by a set of stakeholders, and views that specify a query of the model, and a rendering of the query results, that is intended to satisfy a particular viewpoint (see 7.26 ).
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of viewpoints that specify information of interest by a set of stakeholders, and views that specify a query of the model, and a rendering of the query results, that is intended to satisfy a particular viewpoint (see 7.26 ).

Equivalent (JP): ユーザー定義メタデータ
Definition (EN): The modeling of user-defined metadata that allows for both simple tagging of elements with additional model-level information and more sophisticated semantic extension of the SysML language.
Context/Example (EN): The modeling of user-defined metadata that allows for both simple tagging of elements with additional model-level information and more sophisticated semantic extension of the SysML language. (See 7.27 .)
